hub_hero_subtitle: Tap it, light it, let it tick.
hub_hero_substatement: Sounds, visuals, clocks, timers, stickers, extensions — an interactive studio that builds things with a beat you can tap.
hub_works_item_spv2_desc: A Chrome extension that listens to YouTube and draws what it hears. Live on the Web Store.
hub_works_item_galton_timer_desc: A Galton-board timer where balls fall into a bell curve. Watch time pile up as physics.
hub_works_item_wall_clock_desc: A browser that becomes a wall clock. Burn-in shift and circadian color, built in from day one.
hub_works_item_galton_tempo_desc: A rhythm generator that fires grains on the beat and lets gravity decide the swing.
hub_works_item_vogel_timer_desc: Time does not tick down, it fills up. A golden-angle spiral blooms while the clock runs.
hub_works_item_fractal_beat_desc: A visualizer where fractals grow to the beat. Still feeling out the geometry of music.
hub_works_item_lp_stamps_desc: LINE sticker slot, in preparation. Taking the studio's characters out to another channel.
hub_lab_body: Before it taps, lights, or ticks — the shelf where unfinished sketches sit out, still warm, where you can touch them.
lab_meta_description: The workbench, mid-build. Drafts of motion, beat, and light — left out where you can touch them.
lab_lede_body: Not the showroom — the shelf where unfinished sketches sit. Pieces that survive eventually slip into something in Works.
lab_001_intro: Three takes on a slow-moving backdrop, one per technique. Drag the sliders to find a speed and amplitude you like.
lab_001_a_caption: Pure CSS gradients and transforms. Light, honest, nowhere to hide.
lab_001_b_caption: SVG feTurbulence, raising the grain. Color edges fogged with sand.
lab_001_c_caption: A fragment shader drawing one continuous field. Heaviest of the three, and the one that reaches furthest.
lab_002_intro: Colors shift inside the tank — pale gold, cloudy white, deep amber. Which one pours is yours to choose. A look inside the moment before a Tipsy Tap pour. The long version that keeps cycling is fermenting behind the studio itself.
lab_002_a_caption: Three beers, switched by hand to compare the color and warmth just before the tap. A single fragment shader. The auto-cycling version simmers as the studio's backdrop.
lab_002_switch_hint: Use arrow keys to switch beer
lab_footer_note: Sketches that grow up move over to Works. Take any fragment you like with you — it's MIT.
spv_hero_subtitle: Five real-time meters for reading sound — waveform, spectrum, notes, BPM, and key — right in your browser's side panel.
spv_demo_lede: Watch all five meters react to live sound.
spv_modules_lede: Each meter reads one dimension of sound — built to be read, not just admired.
spv_modules_oscilloscope_desc: Watch the raw waveform over time. Switch between Waveform and Lissajous (XY), tune the time and vertical scale, and let auto-trigger lock the trace to check phase and stereo image.
spv_modules_spectrum_desc: A 32-band logarithmic FFT lays out the whole spectrum from lows to highs. Peak-hold and frequency labels make it easy to spot a muddy low end or a harsh top.
spv_modules_chromagram_desc: Detects the pitch classes sounding right now — polyphonically — and maps them onto a piano keyboard. Note Flow scrolls the harmony past as a moving ribbon.
spv_modules_bpm_desc: Picks up onsets with spectral flux and estimates tempo by autocorrelation. The track's BPM reads out live — a quick reference for beatmatching or transcribing.
spv_modules_key_desc: Estimates the key from the chroma profile and shows it in DJ-friendly Camelot notation — so you can pick a harmonically matching next track in seconds.
spv_modules_trackinfo_desc: Title, channel, BPM, and key in one compact readout — the identity of whatever's playing, always next to the meters.
spv_themes_body: Ten built in — Default, Neon, Monochrome, Amber, Phosphor, Sakura and more — plus Strudel-derived variants for 40+ in total. Recolor the meters to match your mood or the lighting in the booth.
spv_themes_strudel: Strudel auto-sync: when you're capturing strudel.cc, the meters automatically follow your editor's theme.
spv_sites_lede: Works out of the box across the major music and video services — and extends to anywhere you need.
spv_sites_all: + All websites — enable any site you like under Settings › Advanced.
spv_sites_drm_note: Note: DRM-protected streams (such as Spotify Premium, Apple Music subscriptions, or Tidal HiFi) may not be capturable — this is a browser-side limitation.
spv_privacy_lede: SPV-2 doesn't touch the page, doesn't track you, and doesn't send anything out. Audio is captured via Chrome's tabCapture API and processed entirely inside the extension.
spv_privacy_point_1_desc: Nothing is injected into the page's DOM. The site you're viewing stays exactly as it is.
spv_privacy_point_2_desc: No analytics, no telemetry, no ads. None of your listening is collected.
spv_privacy_point_3_desc: No outbound requests at all. Everything happens on your machine.
spv_privacy_tech_note: Manifest V3 · audio processed in an Offscreen Document · open source under the MIT license.
spv_principles_zeroinjection_desc: Never touch the page — no content scripts, no DOM injection.
spv_principles_localbydefault_desc: Everything runs locally — no servers, no telemetry.
spv_principles_onepanel_desc: All the meters live in one side panel — no tab-switching.
spv_releases_lede: Since launch, the meters, supported sites, themes, and languages have grown steadily.
spv_release_v100_note: First release. Five meters (Oscilloscope, Spectrum, Chromagram, BPM, Key) plus Track Info, ten themes, and a customizable layout.
spv_release_v110_note: Multi-site support. Added 10+ sites beyond YouTube, an All-websites mode, a vertical scale for the Oscilloscope, and a confirmation step before the permissions dialog.
spv_release_v120_note: Strudel integration. Added 41 Strudel-derived themes; on strudel.cc the meters auto-sync to your editor theme. New theme picker, too.
spv_release_v130_note: Japanese and German localization (both the extension UI and the Web Store listing).
spv_release_v131_note: Smoother low-end in the Spectrum view: the lowest bars no longer flatten out, now interpolated cleanly down to 20 Hz. Spanish localization added.
spv_finalcta_body: Open the side panel and the song you're already playing comes alive on the meters.
spv_footer_tagline: One of Tipsy Tap Studio's interactive experiments.
